audio.h(3) Library Functions Manual audio.h(3)

NAME

audio.h - Stub audio types for WASM builds (uses Web Audio API instead of PortAudio).

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct audio_batch_info_t
Parsed audio batch packet header information. struct audio_frame_t

Functions


asciichat_error_t audio_parse_batch_header (const void *data, size_t len, audio_batch_info_t *out_batch)
asciichat_error_t audio_validate_batch_params (const audio_batch_info_t *batch)
bool audio_is_supported_sample_rate (uint32_t sample_rate)

Detailed Description

Stub audio types for WASM builds (uses Web Audio API instead of PortAudio).

This header provides minimal audio type definitions for WASM builds. In WASM, audio is handled by Web Audio API via AudioPipeline.ts instead of PortAudio. This stub provides just enough definitions for network packet parsing to compile.

Definition in file lib/platform/wasm/stubs/audio.h.

Function Documentation

bool audio_is_supported_sample_rate (uint32_t sample_rate)

Definition at line 2074 of file lib/audio/audio.c.

2074                                                           {
2075   // List of commonly supported audio sample rates
2076   static const uint32_t supported_rates[] = {
2077       8000,   // Telephone quality
2078       16000,  // Wideband telephony
2079       24000,  // High quality speech
2080       32000,  // Good for video
2081       44100,  // CD quality (less common in VoIP)
2082       48000,  // Standard professional
2083       96000,  // High-end professional
2084       192000, // Ultra-high-end mastering
2085   };
2086 
2087   const size_t rate_count = sizeof(supported_rates) / sizeof(supported_rates[0]);
2088   for (size_t i = 0; i < rate_count; i++) {
2089     if (sample_rate == supported_rates[i]) {
2090       return true;
2091     }
2092   }
2093 
2094   return false;
2095 }








asciichat_error_t audio_parse_batch_header (const void *
  data, size_t len, audio_batch_info_t * out_batch)


Definition at line 2008 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


2008                                                                                                         {
2009   if (!data) {
2010     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch header data pointer is NULL");
2011   }
2012 
2013   if (!out_batch) {
2014     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch info output pointer is NULL");
2015   }
2016 
2017   if (len < sizeof(audio_batch_packet_t)) {
2018     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch header too small (len=%zu, expected=%zu)", len,
2019                      sizeof(audio_batch_packet_t));
2020   }
2021 
2022   const audio_batch_packet_t *batch_header = (const audio_batch_packet_t *)data;
2023 
2024   // Unpack network byte order values to host byte order
2025   out_batch->batch_count = ntohl(batch_header->batch_count);
2026   out_batch->total_samples = ntohl(batch_header->total_samples);
2027   out_batch->sample_rate = ntohl(batch_header->sample_rate);
2028   out_batch->channels = ntohl(batch_header->channels);
2029 
2030   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2031 }










asciichat_error_t audio_validate_batch_params (const
  audio_batch_info_t * batch)


Definition at line 2033 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


2033                                                                                {
2034   if (!batch) {
2035     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch info pointer is NULL");
2036   }
2037 
2038   // Validate batch_count
2039   if (batch->batch_count == 0) {
2040     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch count cannot be zero");
2041   }
2042 
2043   // Check for reasonable max (256 frames per batch is very generous)
2044   if (batch->batch_count > 256) {
2045     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch count too large (batch_count=%u, max=256)", batch->batch_count);
2046   }
2047 
2048   // Validate channels (1=mono, 2=stereo, max 8 for multi-channel)
2049   if (batch->channels == 0 || batch->channels > 8) {
2050     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid channel count (channels=%u, valid=1-8)", batch->channels);
2051   }
2052 
2053   // Validate sample rate
2054   if (!audio_is_supported_sample_rate(batch->sample_rate)) {
2055     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Unsupported sample rate (sample_rate=%u)", batch->sample_rate);
2056   }
2057 
2058   // Check for reasonable sample counts
2059   if (batch->total_samples == 0) {
2060     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch has zero samples");
2061   }
2062 
2063   // Each batch typically has samples_per_frame worth of samples
2064   // For 48kHz at 20ms per frame: 48000 * 0.02 = 960 samples per frame
2065   // With max 256 frames, that's up to ~245k samples per batch
2066   if (batch->total_samples > 1000000) {
2067     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch sample count suspiciously large (total_samples=%u)",
2068                      batch->total_samples);
2069   }
2070 
2071   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2072 }
